questions.q: Who was Shurpanakha's most famous brother?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha was the sister of Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka.

questions.q: What kind of being was Shurpanakha?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha was a rakshasi, a demoness, and sister of the demon king Ravana.

questions.q: Whom did Shurpanakha first become infatuated with in the forest?
questions.explanation: When she encountered the exiles, Shurpanakha was first smitten with the handsome Rama.

questions.q: Who disfigured Shurpanakha by cutting off her nose and ears?
questions.explanation: At Rama's bidding, Lakshmana cut off Shurpanakha's nose and ears.

questions.q: In which forest region did Shurpanakha meet Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: The encounter took place at Panchavati, the grove on the Godavari where the exiles had built their hut.

questions.q: Why did Rama refuse Shurpanakha's proposal of marriage?
options: He had taken a vow of celibacy
He was already married to Sita
He did not speak her language
questions.explanation: Rama told her he was already married to Sita and so could not accept her.

questions.q: To whom did Shurpanakha run for help after being disfigured?
questions.explanation: Humiliated and bleeding, Shurpanakha fled to incite her brothers and ultimately Ravana.

questions.q: Toward whom did Shurpanakha lunge in jealous rage, prompting her disfigurement?
questions.explanation: Enraged that Rama loved Sita, Shurpanakha attacked Sita, and Lakshmana then mutilated her.

questions.q: Shurpanakha's grievance ultimately drew which great enemy against Rama?
questions.explanation: Her complaint inflamed Ravana, leading him to abduct Sita and wage war against Rama.

questions.q: When Rama declined her, to whom did Shurpanakha then turn her advances?
questions.explanation: After Rama refused, Shurpanakha approached Lakshmana, who also rebuffed her mockingly.

questions.q: What was Shurpanakha's appearance when she first approached Rama?
options: She came in her true monstrous form
She assumed a beautiful, alluring form
She appeared as an old beggar woman
She came as a golden deer
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha used her power to take on a charming and beautiful form to attract Rama.

questions.q: Which demon brother of Shurpanakha first marched against Rama after her disfigurement?
questions.explanation: She first went to Khara of Janasthana, whose forces Rama then destroyed.

questions.q: How many rakshasa warriors did Rama slay at Janasthana after Shurpanakha incited them?
questions.explanation: Rama single-handedly destroyed Khara's army of fourteen thousand rakshasas at Janasthana.

questions.q: Besides Khara, which other brother of Shurpanakha was slain in the battle of Janasthana?
questions.explanation: Dushana, along with Khara and Trishira, was killed by Rama at Janasthana.

questions.q: To rouse Ravana, what did Shurpanakha emphasize about Sita?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha described Sita's surpassing beauty to inflame Ravana's desire and revenge.

questions.q: What is the literal meaning of the name 'Shurpanakha'?
options: One whose nails are like winnowing baskets
questions.explanation: The name Shurpanakha means 'she whose fingernails are like winnowing fans (shurpa).'

questions.q: Who was Shurpanakha's father, the sage father of Ravana's line?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha was the daughter of the sage Vishravas (son of Pulastya) and the demoness Kaikasi.

questions.q: After fleeing Lakshmana, where did Shurpanakha go to lament before reaching Ravana?
questions.explanation: She first went to Khara's stronghold at Janasthana before later traveling to Lanka to rouse Ravana.

questions.q: Which of these was NOT a brother of Shurpanakha?
questions.explanation: Sugriva was the vanara king, not a brother of Shurpanakha; her brothers included Ravana, Kumbhakarna, and Vibhishana.

questions.q: How did Lakshmana initially respond when Shurpanakha proposed to him?
options: He mocked her, saying he was a mere servant
questions.explanation: Lakshmana playfully mocked her, calling himself a servant and sending her back toward Rama.

questions.q: What plan did Shurpanakha's complaint ultimately set in motion against the exiles?
options: The abduction of Sita by Ravana
questions.explanation: Her incitement led Ravana to plot and carry out the abduction of Sita from Panchavati.

questions.q: Which Book (Kanda) of the Valmiki Ramayana contains the Shurpanakha episode?
questions.explanation: The Shurpanakha episode occurs in the Aranya Kanda, the 'forest' book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: According to tradition, who was Shurpanakha's husband, slain by Ravana?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Shurpanakha's husband was the danava Vidyutjihva, killed by Ravana, fueling her own bitterness.

questions.q: What was another name by which Shurpanakha is known in some traditions?
questions.explanation: In some southern traditions Shurpanakha is called Meenakshi, 'one with fish-shaped eyes.'

questions.q: Who was the mother of Shurpanakha and Ravana?
questions.explanation: Shurpanakha and Ravana were born to Kaikasi, the demoness wife of the sage Vishravas.

questions.q: Which third brother, killed at Janasthana, is named alongside Khara and Dushana?
questions.explanation: Trishira ('three-headed') was slain by Rama at Janasthana together with Khara and Dushana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana narrative, what immediate purpose did Shurpanakha's mutilation serve in the plot?
options: It triggered the chain of events leading to Ravana's war
It ended the forest exile early
It freed Jatayu from a curse
questions.explanation: Her disfigurement set off the rakshasa retaliation, Sita's abduction, and ultimately the war with Ravana.

questions.q: Which sage's hermitage region of Dandaka forest was near where Shurpanakha roamed?
questions.explanation: The exiles settled at Panchavati on the advice of the sage Agastya, in the Dandaka forest where Shurpanakha roamed.

questions.q: What did Shurpanakha claim she wanted to do with Sita when she first lunged at her?
options: Devour her to remove her rival
questions.explanation: In jealous fury Shurpanakha rushed to devour Sita, whom she saw as the obstacle to winning Rama.

questions.q: What was the name of the kingdom from which Shurpanakha summoned her brother Khara's forces?
questions.explanation: Khara ruled Janasthana, a rakshasa outpost in the Dandaka forest, from which his fourteen thousand warriors marched.
